# harness-kit

> One canonical knowledge base for every coding agent — Claude Code, Cursor,
> Codex, OpenCode — with CI that fails when it drifts. A Claude Code
> skill/plugin that scaffolds a standardized, enforced cross-agent harness
> (docs, skills, hooks, permissions, quality gates) into any repository,
> vendoring everything so the target repo depends on nothing at runtime.

The repository root is itself a live installation of the kit (dogfooding);
only the `plugins/harness-kit/` directory ships to users.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): value proposition, install, layout, status
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): table of contents of this repo's own knowledge base
- [The harness pattern](plugins/harness-kit/skills/harness-kit/references/pattern.md): design principles, anatomy, the three layers, upgrade model
- [Provider matrix](plugins/harness-kit/skills/harness-kit/references/provider-matrix.md): per-harness file locations, hook events, payloads — key facts carry verification stamps, with a Sources section to re-check against
- [Architecture](docs/architecture/ARCHITECTURE.md): how this repo embodies the pattern
- [Decision records](docs/architecture/decisions/README.md): why advisory stop-hooks, vendoring, generated stubs, verification stamps, manifest self-protection

## Usage

- [The skill](plugins/harness-kit/skills/harness-kit/SKILL.md): init / audit / add-skill / add-agent / add-hook / update workflows
- [Contributing](CONTRIBUTING.md): quality gates, template discipline
- [Changelog](CHANGELOG.md)
